title: Combine Automated and Manual Accessibility Evidence
summary: Build and verify accessibility test strategy in community survey release audit through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain accessibility test strategy from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply accessibility test strategy without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue and harden the Community Needs Survey artifact here. -->

steps.title: 01 · Predict the community survey release audit case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Which claim preserves accessibility test strategy when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: community survey release audit.
steps.why: Combine Automated and Manual Accessibility Evidence makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 1 in community survey release audit.

content.title: community survey release audit evidence
content.text: Choose the claim that preserves purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Combine Automated and Manual Accessibility Evidence checkpoint 1: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Combine Automated and Manual Accessibility Evidence checkpoint 1: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Combine Automated and Manual Accessibility Evidence checkpoint 1: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Community survey release audit case 1: a perfect automated score proves conformance and usable access.
Community survey release audit case 1: use automation as one evidence source, run task-based manual and assistive checks, document environments, and retest affected paths after repair.
Community survey release audit case 1: skip automated checks because only manual review can ever produce useful evidence.

options.text: community survey release audit phase 4.1: define representative tasks, risks, environments, and success criteria
community survey release audit phase 4.2: run validation and automation to locate machine-detectable failures
community survey release audit phase 4.3: run keyboard, zoom, preference, changed-state, and assistive-technology checks
community survey release audit phase 4.4: record evidence, repair root causes, and rerun all affected tasks

options.text: Community survey release audit case 5: keep the community survey release audit defect because the happy path worked once.

Community survey release audit case 5: a plausible success state is incomplete evidence. Use automation as one evidence source, run task-based manual and assistive checks, document environments, and retest affected paths after repair.
Community survey release audit case 5: replace the complete artifact before isolating the failed relationship.

steps.title: 06 · Read the community survey release audit case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 6: Which claim preserves accessibility test strategy when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: community survey release audit.
steps.why: Combine Automated and Manual Accessibility Evidence makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 6 in community survey release audit.

content.text: Automated checks quickly find some machine-detectable failures but cannot prove usable access or conformance. A strong strategy combines validation and automation with keyboard, zoom, reflow, forced colors, reduced motion, screen-reader sampling, changed content, error recovery, and human judgment. Reports distinguish tool results from manual observations and record environment plus reproduction steps. Apply it to community survey release audit.

hints: Combine Automated and Manual Accessibility Evidence checkpoint 6: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Combine Automated and Manual Accessibility Evidence checkpoint 6: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Combine Automated and Manual Accessibility Evidence checkpoint 6: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Community survey release audit case 6: a perfect automated score proves conformance and usable access.
Community survey release audit case 6: use automation as one evidence source, run task-based manual and assistive checks, document environments, and retest affected paths after repair.
Community survey release audit case 6: skip automated checks because only manual review can ever produce useful evidence.
